skill.name: alphafold-db
skill.description: Access over 200M protein structures from AlphaFold DB; use when you need to retrieve predicted 3D structures (PDB/mmCIF), confidence metrics (pLDDT/PAE), or protein metadata by UniProt accession.
skill.summary: Download predicted 3D protein structures from AlphaFold DB using UniProt accession IDs and retrieve confidence metrics.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 168 static findings evaluated as false positives or legitimate operations for the skill's purpose. The 5 HIGH-severity 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are caused by version strings (v4) and identifiers in URLs/file contents, not actual crypto usage. The CRITICAL 'Windows SAM database' and 'SQLite database' findings are false positives from biological residue identifiers (e.g., SAM = S-adenosylmethionine) in mmCIF protein structure data. The MEDIUM 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown documentation examples, not executable code. Network and filesystem operations are the intended core functionality. No prompt injection or malicious intent detected. Skill is safe to publish with LOW risk.

medium_findings.title: Shell command examples in documentation (false positive - code examples only)
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 26 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in references/api_reference.md and SKILL.md. All instances are shell command examples inside markdown code blocks showing users how to run the skill. No actual code execution occurs from these documentation examples. FALSE POSITIVE.

medium_findings.title: Suspicious combination heuristic (false positive - legitimate operations)
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Code execution + Network + Credential access' and 'Filesystem + Credentials + Network' as heuristic findings. The network calls target only alphafold.ebi.ac.uk, filesystem writes go to user-specified output directories, and no credential access exists. The heuristic was triggered by multiple risk factor categories co-occurring, but all operations are legitimate for the skill's stated purpose. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm (false positive - version strings and URL identifiers)
low_findings.description: 5 HIGH-severity findings for 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at various file locations. These are false positives caused by the scanner matching version strings (e.g., 'v4' in `AF-P00520-F1-model_v4.cif`), the 'MIT' license identifier, and URL path segments. No actual cryptographic weakness exists.

low_findings.title: Windows SAM database and SQLite database (false positive - biological data in mmCIF)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 3 CRITICAL 'Windows SAM database' and many MEDIUM 'SQLite database file' findings in out/P00520.cif. These are false positives. The mmCIF (mmCIF) format file contains biological residue identifiers where 'SAM' refers to S-adenosylmethionine, a common protein ligand. The structured data sections resemble database formatting but are standard biological structure data.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance (false positive - protein residue identifiers)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged over 80 LOW-severity 'System reconnaissance' findings in out/P00520.cif. These are residue identifiers like SER, THR, LYS, chain IDs, and coordinate data that are standard mmCIF format elements for describing protein 3D structures. No reconnaissance behavior exists.

low_findings.title: C2 keywords (false positive - normal biological data)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'C2 keywords' at out/P00520.cif:68 as a HIGH finding. This is standard mmCIF structural data, not command-and-control indicators. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs and HTTP libraries (legitimate - AlphaFold DB API access)
low_findings.description: Scanner found hardcoded URLs and Python HTTP library usage across scripts/fetch_structure.py, SKILL.md, references/api_reference.md, and output files. All URLs point to the official AlphaFold DB at alphafold.ebi.ac.uk. HTTP requests are the intended mechanism for fetching protein structure data. These are legitimate operations for this skill.

content.user_title: Fetch and Analyze AlphaFold Protein Structures
content.value_statement: Researchers need quick access to predicted protein structures for their work. This skill downloads 3D protein structures and confidence data from AlphaFold DB using any valid UniProt accession ID.
actual_capabilities: Download 3D protein structures from AlphaFold DB in mmCIF or PDB format using a UniProt accession ID.
Retrieve protein metadata including gene name, organism, taxonomy ID, and structure version.
Access confidence metrics such as pLDDT scores and PAE (Predicted Aligned Error) data.
Save structure files and metadata JSON to a local directory for downstream analysis.
Support batch processing by accepting UniProt IDs as command-line arguments.
limitations: Requires a valid UniProt accession ID. Not all proteins have AlphaFold predictions.
Only supports AlphaFold DB as the data source. Does not query PDB, SWISS-MODEL, or other databases.
Does not analyze or visualize structures. Downloads files only for use with other tools.
The AlphaFold DB API may have rate limits for large batch requests.

use_cases.title: Drug Discovery Researcher
use_cases.description: Download predicted structures for target proteins to support drug docking studies and binding site analysis.

use_cases.title: Structural Biology Student
use_cases.description: Retrieve protein structures for coursework, lab reports, or thesis work involving protein folding analysis.

use_cases.title: Bioinformatics Pipeline Developer
use_cases.description: Integrate structure fetching into automated pipelines that process large protein datasets for comparative analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch a Single Protein Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Download the AlphaFold structure for the human ABL1 kinase (UniProt: P00520) in mmCIF format and save it to the ./structures directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs one protein structure for individual analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve Confidence Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the complete metadata including pLDDT confidence scores and PAE data URL for UniProt protein P00520 and explain what the confidence scores mean for this protein.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to assess prediction reliability before analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Multiple Structures
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch AlphaFold structures for proteins P00520, P04637, and P01112 in PDB format. Compare their confidence metrics and identify which regions are most reliably predicted.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to compare structure quality across multiple proteins.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Analysis Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Download structures for 10 cancer-related kinases by UniProt ID, save all to ./cancer_kinases/, and generate a CSV summary of their metadata including gene names, organisms, and structure versions.
prompt_templates.scenario: User is building an automated pipeline for processing many proteins.

output_examples.input: Fetch the AlphaFold structure for P00520 in mmCIF format.
output_examples.output: The skill downloads the ABL1 tyrosine-protein kinase structure as a .cif file and a .json metadata file. The metadata includes gene name ABL1, organism Homo sapiens, taxonomy ID 9606, and URLs for the structure and PAE data files.

output_examples.input: Get the PAE confidence data for protein P04637 (TP53).
output_examples.output: The skill downloads the structure file and metadata JSON. The metadata contains the paeDocUrl field pointing to the Predicted Aligned Error JSON file, which can assess inter-domain confidence for the p53 tumor suppressor protein.

output_examples.input: Download structures for proteins P00520 and P04637 in PDB format for use with PyMOL.
output_examples.output: Both structures are downloaded as PDB format files and saved to the output directory. Each file contains the full predicted 3D coordinates for the protein. A metadata JSON file is also saved for each protein.

best_practices: Always check the metadata JSON for pLDDT confidence scores to assess prediction reliability before using structures in downstream analysis.
Use mmCIF format by default for better data completeness. Switch to PDB only when compatibility with legacy tools requires it.
Verify the UniProt accession ID is correct and exists in AlphaFold DB before running batch operations on many proteins.
anti_patterns: Assuming all protein regions have high confidence. The pLDDT scores vary per residue, so always review confidence data before drawing conclusions.
Fetching structures one by one for large datasets. Use automated scripts with a list of UniProt IDs instead for efficiency.
Overwriting output files by reusing the same output directory. Organize downloads by project or date to avoid data loss.

faq.question: What is AlphaFold DB?
faq.answer: AlphaFold DB is a public database of over 200 million protein structures predicted by DeepMind's AlphaFold AI system. It is hosted by EMBL-EBI.

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a valid UniProt accession ID. You can find these on the UniProt website (uniprot.org) for any known protein.

faq.question: What file formats are available?
faq.answer: mmCIF (default) and PDB. The mmCIF format contains more complete metadata. Use --format pdb for compatibility with older tools.

faq.question: What is pLDDT?
faq.answer: pLDDT (predicted Local Distance Difference Test) is a per-residue confidence score from 0 to 100. Scores above 90 indicate very high confidence. Scores below 50 indicate low confidence.

faq.question: What is PAE?
faq.answer: PAE (Predicted Aligned Error) measures confidence in the relative positions of residue pairs. It helps assess whether domain-level arrangements are reliable.

faq.question: Does this work for any UniProt ID?
faq.answer: No. AlphaFold DB covers most proteins from key species but may not have predictions for every protein, especially from less-studied organisms.
